revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sakunado_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sakunado_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=佐久奈度神社| 所在地=滋賀県大津市大石中町56| 祭神=瀬織津姫命・速秋津姫命
気吹戸主命・速佐須良姫命| 社格=式内社（名神大）
県社| 創建=天智天皇8年| 例祭=[[4月16日]]・[[7月31日]]| 神事=御手洗祭}} '''佐久奈度神社'''（さくなどじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中町にある神社である。祭神は祓戸四神である。旧社格は県社。 ==ご祭神== * [[瀬織津姫命]] * [[速秋津姫命]] * [[気吹戸主命]] * [[速佐須良姫命]] ==ご神紋== * 心櫻十六菊 ==歴史== [[天智天皇]]8年勅願により、[[中臣金連]]が祓戸の神を祀ったのが創始である。以来、[[七瀬の祓い]]所の一つとして[[貞観]]元年には従五位上の神階を賜った。[[式内社]][[名神大社]]である。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格し、[[大正]]10年に県社に昇格した。 ==祭事== * 4月16日　例祭 * 7月31日　御手洗祭 ==境内社== * 焼鎌神社 * 敏鎌神社 * 稲荷神社 * 八幡神社 ==ご神徳== * 祓い浄め * 武運長久 * 家内安全 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さくなとしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:大津市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=佐久奈度神社| 所在地=滋賀県大津市大石中町56| 祭神=瀬織津姫命・速秋津姫命
気吹戸主命・速佐須良姫命| 社格=式内社（名神大）
県社| 創建=天智天皇8年| 例祭=[[4月16日]]・[[7月31日]]| 神事=御手洗祭}} '''佐久奈度神社'''（さくなどじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中町にある神社である。祭神は[[祓戸大神]]である。旧社格は県社。 ==ご祭神== * [[瀬織津姫命]] * [[速秋津姫命]] * [[気吹戸主命]] * [[速佐須良姫命]] ==ご神紋== * 心櫻十六菊 ==歴史== [[天智天皇]]8年勅願により、[[中臣金連]]が祓戸の神を祀ったのが創始である。以来、[[七瀬の祓い]]所の一つとして[[貞観]]元年には従五位上の神階を賜った。[[式内社]][[名神大社]]である。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格し、[[大正]]10年に県社に昇格した。 ==祭事== * 4月16日　例祭 * 7月31日　御手洗祭 ==境内社== * 焼鎌神社 * 敏鎌神社 * 稲荷神社 * 八幡神社 ==ご神徳== * 祓い浄め * 武運長久 * 家内安全 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さくなとしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:大津市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

佐久奈度神社}} '''佐久奈度神社'''（さくなどじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中町にある神社である。祭神は[[祓戸大神]]である。旧社格は県社。 ==ご祭神== * [[瀬織津姫命]] * [[速秋津姫命]] * [[気吹戸主命]] * [[速佐須良姫命]] ==ご神紋== * 心櫻十六菊 ==歴史== :[[天智天皇]]8年勅願により、[[中臣金連]]が祓戸の神を祀ったのが創始である。以来、[[七瀬の祓い]]所の一つとして[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年には従五位上の神階を賜った。[[式内社]][[名神大社]]である。[[明治]]9年に村社に列格し、[[大正]]10年に県社に昇格した。 ==祭事== * 4月16日　例祭 * 7月31日　御手洗祭 ==境内社== * 焼鎌神社 * 敏鎌神社 * 稲荷神社 * 八幡神社 ==ご神徳== * 祓い浄め * 武運長久 * 家内安全 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] == 外部サイト == [http://sakunado.jp/ 佐久奈度神社]（公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくなとしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 佐久奈度神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|佐久奈度神社]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 滋賀県大津市大石中一丁目2-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 瀬織津姫命
速秋津姫命
気吹戸主命
速佐須良姫命 |社格 = 式内社（名神大）
旧県社 |創建 = 天智天皇8年（669年） |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = [[4月16日]] |神事 = 御手洗祭（7月31日） }} '''佐久奈度神社'''（さくなどじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中町に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[名神大社|式内名神大社]]で[[社格|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == [[祓戸大神]]と総称される以下の4柱を祀る。 * [[瀬織津姫命]] * [[速秋津姫命]] * [[気吹戸主命]] * [[速佐須良姫命]] 祓い浄めを始め、武運長久・家内安全の神徳を有すとされる。 == 神紋 == * 心櫻十六菊 == 歴史 == [[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]）勅願により、[[中臣金]]が祓戸の神を祀ったのが創始である。以来、[[七瀬の祓い]]所の一つとして重んぜられ、[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）[[名神大社#名神の称|名神]]に列し『[[文徳天皇実録]]』同年6月甲寅（13日）条。同条に「散久難度神を明神に列す」とある。、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）には従五位上の[[神階]]を賜り『[[日本三代実録]]』同年1月27日条。、名神大社として[[延喜式神名帳|延喜の制]]に定められるなど当地方の名社であった。 [[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に[[村社]]に列格し、同41年[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]の指定を受け、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）には県社に昇格した。 == 祭事 == * 4月16日 例祭 * 7月31日 御手洗祭 == 境内社 == * 焼鎌神社 * 敏鎌神社 * 稲荷神社 * 八幡神社 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[http://sakunado.jp/ 佐久奈度神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 佐久奈度神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくなとしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:府県社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

拝殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中一丁目2-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[瀬織津姫命]]

{{神社 |名称 = 佐久奈度神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|佐久奈度神社]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中一丁目2-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[瀬織津姫命]]
[[速秋津姫命]]
気吹戸主命
速佐須良姫命 |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]） |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = [[4月16日]] |神事 = 御手洗祭（[[7月31日]]） }} '''佐久奈度神社'''（さくなどじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]大石中町に鎮座する[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）で、[[社格|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == [[祓戸大神]]と総称される以下の4柱を祀る。 * [[瀬織津姫命]] * [[速秋津姫命]] * [[気吹戸主命]] * [[速佐須良姫命]] 祓い浄めを始め、武運長久・家内安全の神徳を有すとされる。 == 神紋 == * 心櫻十六菊 == 歴史 == [[天智天皇]]8年（[[669年]]）勅願により、[[中臣金]]が祓戸の神を祀ったのが創始である。以来、[[七瀬の祓い]]所の一つとして重んぜられ、[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）[[名神大社#名神の称|名神]]に列し『[[文徳天皇実録]]』同年6月甲寅（13日）条。同条に「散久難度神を明神に列す」とある。、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]元年（[[859年]]）には従五位上の[[神階]]を賜り『[[日本三代実録]]』同年1月27日条。、名神大社として[[延喜式神名帳|延喜の制]]に定められるなど当地方の名社であった。 [[大石良勝]]が武運長久・息災延命を祈願して奉納した絵馬の「板絵著色武者騎馬像」が大津市指定文化財として残されている{{Sfn|大津市歴史博物館|2004|p=36}}。 境内から1 km南にある御旅所社殿は鎌倉時代からあった旧社殿を建て替える際に、その部材を用いて建立されたものである{{Sfn|大津市歴史博物館|2004|p=36}}。 [[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に[[村社]]に列格し、同41年[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]の指定を受け、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）には県社に昇格した。[[昭和]]39年（[[1964年]]）に [[天ケ瀬ダム]]の建設に伴い東寄りの低地から現在地に移った{{Sfn|大津市歴史博物館|2004|p=36}}。 == 祭事 == * 4月16日 例祭 * 7月31日 御手洗祭 == 境内社 == * 焼鎌神社 * 敏鎌神社 * 稲荷神社 * 八幡神社 * 橋守社 == 交通アクセス == * [[京阪バス]]大石小学校前で下車、徒歩約5分{{Sfn|大津市歴史博物館|2004|p=36}} == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大津　歴史と文化　身近な歴史再発見！|date=2004-10-01|year=2004|publisher=大津市|author=大津市歴史博物館|ref=harv}} == 外部リンク == *[http://sakunado.jp/ 佐久奈度神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 佐久奈度神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくなとしんしや}} [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名さくなとしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:中臣氏|社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name= Sakunado Shrine |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}} * {{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: Annual Festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Sakunado Shrine |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}} * {{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: Annual Festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: Annual Festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Sakunado Shrine |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}} * {{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: Annual Festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: Annual Festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}} * {{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}} * {{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}} * {{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

{{Shrine |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]]

Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]]

The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:11]]

These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
