It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:remove new pages from these]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] {{Munakata Faith}} [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akunado Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakunado Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}}

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|12=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|12=draft}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|12=draft}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|12=draft}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|12=draft}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|12=draft}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|12=draft}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=draft}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=draft}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|12=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|12=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|12=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|12=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|12=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}}

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|12=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} |shrine_rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|12=draft|12=draft}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|12=draft|12=draft}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|12=draft|12=draft}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft|12=draft}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|12=draft|12=draft}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|12=draft|12=draft}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|12=draft|12=draft}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|12=draft|12=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|12=draft|12=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|12=draft|12=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|12=draft|12=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sakunado Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Bus|qq=}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 16|qq=}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 31|qq=}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-kuni|qq=}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna|qq=}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Bus|qq=}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 16|qq=}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 31|qq=}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-kuni|qq=}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna|qq=}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Bus|qq=}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan shrines]] [[Category:Sakunado Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Bus|qq=}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} |established= {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= {{ill|April 16|ja|4月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 16|qq=}} |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ({{ill|July 31|ja|7月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 31|qq=}}) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to {{ill|Ne-no-kuni|ja|根の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-kuni|qq=}} (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=}}. In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁寿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna|qq=}} 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * {{ill|Keihan Bus|ja|京阪バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Bus|qq=}}, alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]]
