By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]

The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ōtsu]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Sakunado_jinja.jpg|250px|Sakunado Shrine]]
Worship Hall |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34|54|41.2|N|135|54|45.4|E|scale:25000}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|1=Myojin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1052905}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=|1=Ōishi Yoshikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438256}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム|1=Amagase Dam|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4739121}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=|1=Ōishi Yoshikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438256}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム|1=Amagase Dam|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4739121}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11382603]] Wikidata name: Sakunado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Sakunado jinja.jpg |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34.911444|N|135.912611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) |prefecture= {{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1052905}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=|1=Ōishi Yoshikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438256}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム|1=Amagase Dam|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4739121}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11382603]] Wikidata name: Sakunado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Sakunado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Sakunado jinja.jpg |location= 1-2-1 Oishi-naka, Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture |coordinates= {{Coord|34.911444|N|135.912611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deities= {{ill|Seoritsu-Hime|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}}
{{ill|Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|速秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|1=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11326733}}
{{ill|Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|気吹戸主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|ja|速佐須良姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神大|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9610964}})
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established= [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] 8th year (669 CE) |main_building= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay Shrine) |annual_festival= [[:en:April 16|April 16]] |other_ceremonies= Mitarashi Festival ([[:en:July 31|July 31]]) |prefecture= {{ill|Otsu|qid=Q202907}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Sakunado Shrine''' (佐久奈度神社, ''Sakunado Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ōtsu City, Shiga Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1052905}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day. The entry states: "Sakunado Shrine is elevated to Myojin status." By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 1 (859 CE), it received the Rank of ''Junior Fifth Rank, Upper''.''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', Jōgan 1, 1st month, 27th day. The shrine was a prominent regional religious site, and an equestrian painting known as ''Itabei Choshoku Musha Kiba-zo'' (''Board Painting of an Armored Warrior on Horseback''), donated by {{ill|Oishi Yoshikatsu|ja|大石良勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oishi Yoshikatsu|qq=|1=Ōishi Yoshikatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438256}} as a prayer for victory and longevity, remains a designated cultural asset of Ōtsu City.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Otsu: History and Culture|year=2004|author=Ōtsu City History Museum}} The old shrine building, replaced during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], was moved to the southern ''Mitarashi'' site, 1 km away, using the original materials. In the modern era, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1876, was recognized as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Kanpei-sha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} in 1908, and was elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. It was relocated to its current location in 1964 due to the construction of the {{ill|Amagase Dam|ja|天ケ瀬ダム|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagase Dam|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天ヶ瀬ダム|1=Amagase Dam|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4739121}}. == Festivals == * April 16: festival * July 31: Mitarashi Festival == Associated Shrines == * Yakikama Shrine * Toshikama Shrine * Inari Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Hashimori Shrine == Access == * [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]], alight at ''Ōishi Elementary School-mae'' stop, then a 5-minute walk. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://sakunado.jp/ Sakunado Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=88 Sakunado Shrine] (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association) {{Shinto}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|佐久奈度神社|version=100477195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sakunado Shrine]] [[ja:佐久奈度神社]] [[sn:akunado Shrine]] [[Category:Nakatomi clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Ranked as a Myojin Taisha) and a former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four deities collectively known as {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo-no-Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo-no-Ōkami|qq=}}, associated with purification: * {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} (瀬織津比売) – Carries away all calamities, sins, and impurities to rivers and the sea. * {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|qq=}} (速開都比売神) – Awaits at river mouths or the ocean floor to swallow the impurities. * {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|qq=}} (気吹戸主) – Expels the absorbed impurities to [[:en:Ne-no-kuni|Ne-no-kuni]] (the underworld). * {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasurahime|qq=}} (速佐須良比売) – Wanders through the underworld to dissolve the impurities entirely. These deities are believed to provide blessings of purification, long-lasting fortune in war, and household safety. == Shrine Crest == * ''Shinzakura Juurokugiku'' (Sixteen-petaled Cherry Blossom and Chrysanthemum) == History == Sakunado Shrine was founded in the 8th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]'s reign (669 CE) by imperial order, with {{ill|Nakatomi no Kane|ja|中臣金|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kane|qq=|1=Nakatomi no Kane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366917}} enshrining the deities of Haraedo. It became known as one of the significant purification sites of {{ill|Nana Naru Purification|ja|七瀬の祓い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana Naru Purification|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Ninju|Ninna]] 1 (851 CE), it was elevated to a {{ill|Myojin Taisha|ja|名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin Taisha|qq=|1=myoujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415523}} shrine.''{{ill|Montoku Jitsuroku|ja|文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Montoku Jitsuroku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本文徳天皇実録|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1052905}}'', Ninna 1, 6th month, 13th day.
